div.head: The Chargé in the Soviet Union (Dickerson) to the Secretary of State
div.p:
2098. Embassy’s 1765, October 6, noon. With reference to the Moscow News BulletinApparently a wartime successor to the Moscow News. articleThe article was entitled: “Churchmen Back Country’s War Effort.” on the support being given to the Soviet war effort by the Russian Orthodox Church, excerpts from which are quoted in the Embassy’s 2099Not printed. which will follow this, the Embassy had seen no similar articles in the vernacular press and it is believed that the interview as published represents no more than that lip service to religious freedom to which Ambassador Harriman referred in the Embassy’s telegram No. 1758, October 4, noon. Evidence in illustration of this belief is afforded by the fact that it is known to
the Embassy that the Sofia Cathedral,A famous ancient church in old Novgorod. the desecration of which by the Germans is represented as having so aroused the indignation of Archbishop Andrey’sArchbishop Andrey of the Samara (Kuibyshev) bishopric of the Russian Orthodox Church, of the conservative wing, gave the interview. followers, was being used as a secular museum some years ago. As the Department is aware the Moscow News is published almost exclusively for foreign consumption and from the reference to the American Relief AdministrationArchbishop Andrey had assisted staff members of the American Relief Administration in 1921. Concerning the humanitarian activities of this organization during the famine in the Soviet Union at that time, see footnote 32, p. 730. in the article in question, it may reasonably be inferred that it was designed in particular for American readers.
